description: Comprehensive craft procedures, instrument specs, ISA standards, installation methods, and troubleshooting for instrumentation technicians

isa_tag_structure.format: First letter = measured variable, remaining = function, then loop number
isa_tag_structure.loop_numbering: Loop numbers are typically assigned per P&ID area. Example: FIC-1001 = Flow Indicating Controller, loop 1001. Numbers 1-999 common for small projects, 1000+ for large facilities. Safety loops often use 8000 or 9000 series.

signal_reference.burnout_upscale: Greater than 21.0 mA — sensor failure, transmitter drives high to alert DCS
signal_reference.burnout_downscale: Less than 3.6 mA — sensor failure, transmitter drives low to alert DCS

3_15_psi.note: Old pneumatic standard — still found on some older plants and positioners

fieldbus_signals.HART: Digital signal superimposed on 4-20mA (not instead of)

dp_transmitter.mounting: Mount on 2-inch pipe stand or bracket. Transmitter must be accessible for calibration.
dp_transmitter.orientation: H side = high pressure (process), L side = low pressure (reference). Verify with P&ID.

impulse_lines.liquid: Both taps at same elevation if possible. Slope lines DOWN to transmitter. Transmitter BELOW taps.
impulse_lines.gas: Slope lines UP to transmitter. Transmitter ABOVE taps. Drain legs at low points.
impulse_lines.steam: Condensate pots at BOTH taps. Fill legs with distilled water to same level. Both legs identical length and exposure.
impulse_lines.corrosive: Use diaphragm seals (remote seals) instead of impulse lines. Fill with compatible fill fluid.

dp_transmitter.manifold: Install 3-valve or 5-valve manifold between process and transmitter. ALWAYS equalize before opening to process.
steps: Mount transmitter on bracket/stand at correct elevation
Install manifold — verify H/L orientation
Connect impulse tubing — verify slope direction
Pressure test tubing (1.5x operating or per spec)
Open equalizing valve on manifold
Slowly open HP block valve
Slowly open LP block valve
Close equalizing valve
Verify 4 mA at zero differential
Calibrate per data sheet range
common_mistakes: H and L sides reversed — reading backwards
Impulse lines not sloped — air/liquid traps cause errors
Manifold not equalized before opening to process — overpressure damage
Fill fluid not compatible with process — diaphragm damage

pressure_transmitter.mounting: Process connection on bottom of transmitter. Mount vertically if possible.
steps: Install root valve at process tap
Install instrument isolation valve or needle valve
Mount transmitter on bracket — process connection DOWN for liquids
Connect tubing from isolation valve to transmitter
Pressure test
Open root valve, then isolation valve slowly
Verify reading matches local gauge
Calibrate if needed
pressure_transmitter.gauge_pressure_note: Vent port must be OPEN to atmosphere (not plugged) for gauge pressure transmitters
pressure_transmitter.absolute_pressure_note: Vent port is SEALED — transmitter references internal vacuum

temperature_transmitter.thermowell_first: ALWAYS install thermowell before the element. Process must be depressurized for thermowell installation.
temperature_transmitter.immersion_depth: Minimum 10 times sheath diameter. For 1/2-inch well, minimum 5 inches past pipe wall.
steps: Install thermowell per weld procedure (if welded) or thread into connection
Verify immersion depth meets specification
Apply thermal compound to thermowell bore
Insert TC or RTD element — do NOT force
Wire element to transmitter head — verify polarity for TC
Connect transmitter output wiring
Calibrate transmitter — simulate input at head
tc_rules: Use matching extension wire — J for J, K for K, T for T
NEVER splice different TC types together
NEVER run TC wire in same conduit as power cables
Verify polarity at every junction point
rtd_rules: 3-wire is standard — match lead lengths for accuracy
Do NOT use 2-wire for anything requiring ±0.5°C accuracy
Shield must be grounded at ONE end only

level_transmitter_dp.wet_leg: Reference leg filled with liquid (glycol, silicone, or process fluid). Leg must be kept full. Any loss causes reading error.
level_transmitter_dp.dry_leg: Reference leg open to atmosphere. Simpler but condensation in reference leg causes error.
level_transmitter_dp.electronic_remote_seal: Capillary system filled with silicone or fluorinated fluid. No impulse lines. Temperature affects fill fluid — compensate.
level_transmitter_dp.calculation: Span (inches WC) = Height (inches) × Specific Gravity × 0.0361 (for PSI) or Height × SG directly for inches WC
level_transmitter_dp.suppression_elevation: Suppression: transmitter ABOVE lower tap — zero is suppressed (positive offset). Elevation: transmitter BELOW lower tap — zero is elevated (negative offset).

flow_element_orifice.orientation: Bevel faces DOWNSTREAM. Paddle/handle text faces UPSTREAM. This is the #1 installation error in instrumentation.
flow_element_orifice.straight_runs: Minimum varies by upstream fitting and beta ratio. General rule: 20D upstream / 5D downstream for single 90° elbow. Check AGA/API tables for specific configurations.
flow_element_orifice.tap_location: Flange taps: 1 inch from face. Corner taps: at flange face. Vena contracta: at point of minimum pressure. D and D/2 taps: 1D upstream, D/2 downstream.
flow_element_orifice.drain_vent: Install drain on low side (liquid service) and vent on high side (gas service) of impulse lines
flow_element_orifice.gasket_warning: Gasket must NOT protrude into flow bore — causes measurement error. Use correct ID gaskets.

pre_install_checks: Verify tag number matches P&ID and line spec
Verify body material matches line spec
Check flow direction arrow — install per P&ID
Verify actuator size matches data sheet
Remove ALL shipping stops and blocks from actuator
Verify nameplate data: Cv, trim, packing, actuator type
steps: Set valve on pipe supports — do NOT let pipe support the valve weight
Align flanges — do NOT force gasket alignment with bolts
Install gaskets — verify material and rating match line spec
Torque bolts in star pattern per flange spec
Connect actuator air supply — verify pressure matches nameplate
Stroke valve full range BEFORE connecting to process
Verify fail position matches data sheet (fail-open, fail-closed, fail-last)
Connect positioner feedback linkage
Calibrate positioner: 4 mA = valve position per data sheet, 20 mA = other end
control_valve_installation.fail_position_test: Remove air supply. Valve must go to specified fail position. If fail-close: valve must close tight. If fail-open: valve must open fully.
common_mistakes: Shipping stops not removed — valve won't stroke
Wrong fail position — could be catastrophic in emergency
Air supply pressure wrong — valve won't reach full stroke

type_J.use: General purpose, reducing atmospheres. Iron oxidizes above 540°C.

type_K.use: MOST COMMON general purpose. Good for oxidizing atmospheres.

type_E.use: Highest millivolt output — best for low-temp precision.

type_N.use: Improved version of Type K — better stability, less drift.

type_R.use: High temp, precision. Expensive. Glass, ceramics, steel industries.

type_S.use: Similar to R. Used as reference standard.

rules: Red is ALWAYS negative in US ANSI standard
ALWAYS use matching extension wire type — J wire for J TC, K wire for K TC
NEVER splice different TC types together — creates additional junction
NEVER run TC extension wire in conduit with power cables — EMI noise
Extension wire is cheaper but has narrower temp range than TC wire
At every junction, verify polarity — reversed polarity gives wrong reading

rtd_wiring.2_wire: Least accurate — lead wire resistance adds directly to reading. Only acceptable for short runs (<10 ft) or where ±2°C accuracy is OK.
rtd_wiring.3_wire: Most common industrial configuration. Third wire compensates for lead resistance. Match all 3 wire lengths and gauge.
rtd_wiring.4_wire: Most accurate — eliminates ALL lead resistance effects. Required for laboratory and custody transfer. Uses Kelvin measurement.
rtd_wiring.pt100: 100.00 ohms at 0°C. Alpha = 0.00385 ohms/ohm/°C (IEC 751). Most common worldwide.
rtd_wiring.pt1000: 1000.00 ohms at 0°C. Higher base resistance reduces lead wire error effect. Good for 2-wire applications.

compression_fittings.initial_makeup: 1-1/4 turns past finger tight for 1/2-inch and smaller Swagelok-type. 1-1/8 turns for 3/4-inch and larger.
compression_fittings.remake: Tighten to previous position PLUS 1/4 turn. Mark the nut position before disassembly.
rules: NEVER use Teflon tape on compression fittings — tape gets into ferrule seat and causes leaks
Match fitting material to tubing — 316SS fittings on 316SS tubing
Inspect ferrules before assembly — no nicks, scratches, or deformation
Tubing must be cut square — use a tubing cutter, not a hacksaw
Deburr inside AND outside after cutting
Insert tubing to back of fitting body before tightening
Do NOT mix brands — Swagelok ferrules in Parker bodies (or vice versa) will leak
Maximum number of remakes: typically 3-4 times before ferrule is compressed too thin
compression_fittings.leak_testing: Apply Snoop or soap solution at ambient temp. Bubble = leak. Tighten 1/8 turn increments. If still leaks after 1/2 turn additional, disassemble and inspect.
compression_fittings.bending: Minimum bend radius = 5 times tube OD for 316SS. Use tube bender — do NOT bend by hand or with pliers.

impulse_line_routing.liquid_service: Slope DOWN from process tap to transmitter (1 inch per foot minimum). Transmitter BELOW tap. No traps or high points — air will accumulate and cause errors.
impulse_line_routing.gas_service: Slope UP from process tap to transmitter (1 inch per foot minimum). Transmitter ABOVE tap. No low points where liquid accumulates — condensate causes errors.
impulse_line_routing.steam_service: Install condensate pots at process connection. Fill both legs with distilled water to identical level. Both legs must be same length and same temperature exposure.
impulse_line_routing.corrosive_service: Use diaphragm seals (remote seals) instead of impulse lines. Select fill fluid compatible with process AND ambient temperature range.
general_rules: Route tubing along structural steel — never across walkways or access areas
Use tubing clamps — not wire ties or hose clamps
Label both ends of every tubing run — tag number and destination
Cap all open tubing ends at end of every shift — dirt ingress causes plugging
Pressure test ALL tubing before connecting to instruments (typically 1.5x design pressure)
Blow through with clean dry air or N2 after pressure test — remove debris and moisture

cable_separation.from_power_cables: 12-inch minimum separation in cable tray. 24-inch preferred near VFDs and large motors.
cable_separation.crossing: Cross at 90 degrees only — minimizes electromagnetic coupling
cable_separation.shield_grounding: Ground shield at ONE end only — typically at the marshalling cabinet. Grounding at both ends creates ground loops.
cable_separation.sis_safety: DEDICATED cable tray and conduit for Safety Instrumented System (SIS) wiring — NEVER mixed with BPCS (Basic Process Control System)
cable_separation.is_wiring: Intrinsically Safe circuits: blue cable jacket or blue conduit marking. Separate from non-IS. IS barriers in marshalling cabinet.
cable_separation.tc_extension: Thermocouple extension wire: dedicated conduit or tray. Never in same conduit as 4-20mA signals — EMI crosstalk.

hart_protocol.description: Highway Addressable Remote Transducer. Digital communication superimposed on 4-20mA loop.
requirements: 250-ohm minimum loop resistance for HART communication
HART modem connects at transmitter terminals or across 250-ohm resistor
Loop must have >3.5 mA for communication
Do NOT connect HART modem across power supply — connect across the load resistor
common_parameters: Output trim (match 4 and 20 mA to calibrator)

foundation_fieldbus.description: All-digital protocol replacing 4-20mA. Bus-powered devices on H1 segment.
segment_rules: Maximum cable length: 1900m (6200 ft) without repeater
Trunk and spur topology — NOT daisy-chain
foundation_fieldbus.power: Powered by segment power supply. Calculate voltage drop per device.

3_valve_manifold.purpose: Isolate DP transmitter from process for calibration or removal
to_isolate: Open equalizing valve — transmitter now sees zero DP
Bleed pressure through bleed valve if equipped
3_valve_manifold.critical_rule: NEVER open equalizing valve while both block valves are open on a high-pressure system — full process pressure on both sides of transmitter

5_valve_manifold.purpose: Same as 3-valve plus two bleed/drain valves for venting trapped pressure
5_valve_manifold.advantage: Can safely vent trapped pressure before removing transmitter. Required on high-pressure services.

grounding_and_bonding.instrument_grounding: Single-point ground for each instrument loop — typically at marshalling cabinet or DCS I/O card.
grounding_and_bonding.shield_grounding: Ground at marshalling end only. Float (do NOT ground) at field end. Tape back ungrounded end to prevent accidental contact.
grounding_and_bonding.intrinsically_safe: IS ground bus separate from power ground. IS barriers connected to dedicated IS ground bar. Ground resistance <1 ohm.
grounding_and_bonding.thermocouple: Grounded junction TC: fast response but ground loop risk if transmitter also grounded. Ungrounded junction: slower response but isolated from ground loops. Exposed junction: fastest response, only for clean non-conductive environments.
grounding_and_bonding.mag_meter: Grounding rings or grounding electrodes required. Ground to process piping. Without proper grounding, reading is erratic or zero.
grounding_and_bonding.static_grounding: Bond all instrument tubing and conduit to grounding system in classified areas. Prevents static charge buildup.

steps: Verify instrument is de-energized or LOTO if live process
Connect calibrator to test terminals (or apply known input)
Apply known input at 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%
Record output at each point (should be 4, 8, 12, 16, 20 mA)
Check for linearity — error at each point within tolerance
Tolerance typically ±0.1% of span for transmitters
If out of tolerance: adjust zero first, then span
Repeat 5-point check after adjustment
Record as-found AND as-left values on calibration sheet
Apply calibration sticker with date, initials, next due date
standard_5_point.as_found_rule: ALWAYS record as-found values before making any adjustment. This is your baseline — if instrument was reading correctly, as-found matches as-left. If drifted, as-found shows how far.

smart_transmitter_calibration.sensor_trim: Adjusts the sensor reading to match a known reference (e.g., deadweight tester, temperature bath). This corrects sensor-level errors.
smart_transmitter_calibration.output_trim: Adjusts the 4 mA and 20 mA output to match a precision calibrator. This corrects D/A converter errors.
smart_transmitter_calibration.range_change: Can change URV/LRV via HART without physical recalibration. But sensor trim and output trim still need periodic verification.
smart_transmitter_calibration.note: Smart transmitters self-diagnose. Check diagnostic codes before calibrating — may indicate sensor failure, not drift.

control_valve_calibration.bench_set: Actuator spring range — the air pressure range that strokes the valve. Example: 3-15 PSI or 6-30 PSI.
positioner_calibration: Apply 4 mA — valve should be at starting position (0% or 100% depending on action)
Apply 12 mA — valve should be at 50%
Apply 20 mA — valve should be at ending position
Verify dead band is within spec (typically <1%)
Stroke valve 3 full cycles to check for hysteresis
control_valve_calibration.seat_leakage_test: Class IV (metal seat): 0.01% of rated capacity. Class V: 5 x 10^-4 ml/min per inch of seat diameter. Class VI (soft seat): bubble-tight.

prerequisites: All wiring complete with point-to-point continuity check
DCS/PLC point configured with correct tag, range, units, alarms
Control narrative or logic diagram available
Operations/control room NOTIFIED before any loop test
Safety interlocks properly bypassed with authorization
steps: Verify power to instrument — check 24VDC at field terminals
Apply simulated 4 mA input at field device — verify 0% at DCS
Apply 12 mA — verify 50% at DCS (check engineering units match)
Apply 20 mA — verify 100% at DCS
Verify LOW alarm activates at correct setpoint
Verify HIGH alarm activates at correct setpoint
Verify LOLO and HIHI alarms if configured
For output loops: command 0% from DCS — verify valve at fail position
Command 50% from DCS — verify valve at 50% travel
Command 100% from DCS — verify valve at full stroke
Verify valve position feedback reads correctly in DCS
Remove air supply — verify fail-safe position
For safety loops: verify shutdown activates correctly at trip point
Verify all interlocks function per cause-and-effect matrix
Document all results on loop check sheet — sign and date
Remove bypass on safety interlocks — verify system back to normal
loop_testing.two_person_requirement: Loop testing ALWAYS requires two people: one at the field device, one at the DCS/control room. Use radios for communication.
common_issues: Reversed polarity — shows 0 mA or pegged reading
Wrong DCS channel — signal shows on different tag
Scaling error in DCS — URV/LRV don't match transmitter range
Alarm setpoints not configured — alarms don't activate
Valve responds opposite direction — action (direct/reverse) wrong in positioner or DCS
Safety interlock doesn't trip — logic error, wrong input point, or bypass still in
Position feedback doesn't match — limit switch adjustment needed

orifice_plate_installation.orientation: Bevel faces DOWNSTREAM. Paddle/handle text faces UPSTREAM. This is the #1 installation error in instrumentation.
orifice_plate_installation.straight_runs: Minimum varies by upstream fitting and beta ratio. General rule: 20D upstream / 5D downstream after single 90° elbow. 40D after two elbows in different planes. Flow conditioner can reduce requirements.

tap_types.flange_taps: Most common — 1 inch from each face of orifice flange
tap_types.corner_taps: At flange face — used in Europe
tap_types.D_and_D2: 1D upstream, D/2 downstream — most theoretically accurate

orifice_plate_installation.beta_ratio: d/D where d = orifice bore, D = pipe ID. Typical range 0.3 to 0.7. Below 0.2: too much pressure drop. Above 0.75: poor accuracy.
common_errors: Plate installed backwards — MOST COMMON ERROR, flow reads low/inaccurate
Wrong bore size — wrong orifice plate for this line
Gasket protruding into flow bore — creates turbulence, measurement error
Insufficient straight run — measurement accuracy severely degraded
Drain/vent taps not installed — cannot clear impulse lines
Missing orifice plate data tag — cannot verify bore at turnaround

instrument_air_system.quality: Clean, dry, oil-free. Dew point -40°F or 20°F below ambient (whichever is lower). Particle size <3 micron. Oil content <1 ppm.
instrument_air_system.pressure: Typical 80-100 PSI header. Individual instruments: 20 PSI via filter/regulator. Valve actuators: 60-80 PSI via filter/regulator.
instrument_air_system.test: Blow down all headers and branch lines. Check dew point at furthest point. Verify pressure at each instrument take-off.

cable_testing.megger: Insulation resistance test — minimum 100 MΩ at 500 VDC for instrument cables. Test each conductor to shield, and conductor to conductor.
cable_testing.continuity: Point-to-point continuity check on every wire pair. Verify at marshalling panel and DCS I/O.
cable_testing.hipot: Not typically required for instrument cables unless specified. If required, 1500 VDC for 1 minute.

tubing_pressure_test.test_pressure: 1.5 times design pressure or per project specification
tubing_pressure_test.medium: Clean dry air or nitrogen. NEVER use water for instrument tubing — moisture causes corrosion and freezing.
tubing_pressure_test.hold_time: Typically 10 minutes with no visible drop on gauge
tubing_pressure_test.documentation: Record test pressure, hold time, ambient temperature, pass/fail

heat_tracing_for_instruments.when_required: Process fluid in impulse lines or sensing elements that can freeze, solidify, or become too viscous at ambient temperature.
heat_tracing_for_instruments.electric: Self-regulating heat trace cable (Raychem, Thermon, Nelson). Maintains temperature automatically — increases output as temperature drops.
heat_tracing_for_instruments.steam: Small-bore steam tracer tubing bundled with instrument tubing. Requires steam trap at low point.
rules: Do NOT apply heat trace directly to transmitter electronics — overheat damage
Wrap impulse tubing, not the transmitter body
Use insulation over heat trace — heat trace without insulation wastes energy
Install thermostat or temperature controller to prevent overheating
Label all heat-traced lines — maintenance needs to know before working on them

ground_loop.fix: Ground at ONE end only — marshalling cabinet end

emi.fix: 12-24 inch separation, cross at 90 degrees. Use shielded cable.

loose_connections.fix: Retighten all terminals. Clean corroded connections with contact cleaner. Replace damaged cables.

moisture.cause: Water in junction box or cable
moisture.fix: Dry out JB, replace cable glands, seal conduit entries. Check drain holes.

plugging.symptom: Slow/no response, reading stuck or doesn't track process
plugging.fix: Rod out tubing, blow back with N2, or switch to remote seal. Install self-draining orientation.

condensation.symptom: Reading drifts, erratic in cold weather
condensation.fix: Install drain legs at low points, re-slope tubing, add heat tracing. For gas service — ensure tubing slopes UP.

air_locks.fix: Vent at high points with needle valve or pet cock. Re-slope tubing to eliminate high points.

frozen.symptom: Reading stuck in cold weather, no response to process change
frozen.fix: Thaw carefully with heat gun — NOT open flame. Verify heat trace is energized. Install additional insulation.

leaking.symptom: Reading drifts, Snoop test shows bubbles
leaking.fix: Tighten compression fittings (1/8 turn at a time). Replace ferrules if over-tightened. Check for vibration damage.

common_mistakes.orifice_backwards: Flow reads inaccurate/low → reinstall with bevel downstream, paddle text upstream
common_mistakes.tc_wire_reversed: Temperature reads offset or nonlinear → check polarity at every junction, replace with correct matching extension wire
common_mistakes.mag_meter_vertical_electrodes: Erratic or zero reading → rotate meter body so electrodes are horizontal (3 and 9 o'clock position)
common_mistakes.wrong_range_in_dcs: Proportional error at every point → verify DCS URV/LRV matches transmitter calibrated range exactly
common_mistakes.is_barrier_wrong_type: Loop doesn't power up or reads low → verify barrier voltage and current rating matches transmitter requirements
common_mistakes.manifold_equalize_open: Transmitter reads zero when process is running → check equalizing valve is CLOSED

quality_common_punch_items: Missing or incorrect tag plates
Instrument not level or plumb
Missing sunshade on outdoor transmitters
Tubing supports missing or exceeding maximum spacing
Tubing not properly sloped for service type
Junction box covers not installed or bolts missing
Conduit seal fittings missing in classified areas
Shields grounded at wrong end or both ends
Wrong wire type for thermocouple extension circuits
Missing cable labels at both ends
Calibration not performed or out of tolerance
Loop check not complete or not signed
Control valve stroking issues — shipping stops not removed
Fail-safe action not verified
Missing drain or vent valves on impulse lines
Tubing not pressure tested
Instrument air filter/regulator not installed
Root valve not installed at process tap
Cable tray covers missing in classified areas
IS barriers not installed or wrong type
Grounding connections missing or not tested
As-built redlines not provided
Vendor manuals not in turnover package
Calibration certificates missing from documentation
